Trends & Insights

Baby Naming Trends in Urban vs. Rural Areas (Data)

How baby naming patterns differ between cities and rural areas, with data on uniqueness, tradition, and cultural influence.

By Ziven Borceg8 min readDecember 15, 2025

Where you live significantly influences what you name your baby. Our analysis comparing urban and rural naming patterns across the US reveals striking differences in name choices, diversity, and trend adoption.

Key Differences

Name Diversity

  • Urban areas: Average of 2,800 unique names per 10,000 births
  • Rural areas: Average of 1,900 unique names per 10,000 births
  • Cities show 47% more name diversity than rural communities

    • Trend Adoption Speed

  • Rising names appear in urban areas 2-3 years before rural areas
  • Example: Liam was popular in NYC by 2010 but didn't peak in rural Mississippi until 2016

    • Traditional vs. Modern

  • Rural areas show 35% higher rates of family/legacy naming
  • Urban areas show 60% higher rates of international name adoption

    • Regional Hotspots

    Most Unique-Name-Friendly Cities

  • Portland, OR — Highest name diversity score
  • Austin, TX — Leads in gender-neutral name adoption
  • Brooklyn, NY — Pioneered the vintage revival trend
  • San Francisco, CA — Most international names
  • Nashville, TN — Unique blend of traditional and trendy

    • Most Traditional-Naming Areas

  • Rural Alabama — Highest rate of family names
  • Rural Utah — Religious naming traditions remain strong
  • Rural Appalachia — Anglo-Saxon naming heritage persists
  • Rural Midwest — German/Scandinavian naming traditions continue

    • The Convergence Trend

    Social media and the internet are slowly equalizing urban-rural naming differences. Rural parents now have the same access to naming websites and Instagram trends as urban parents, leading to a gradual convergence in naming patterns.

    Check How Many People Have This Name
